query: South Korea arms export deals 2026, especially Poland K2 tank second executive contract, any deals expected July-August 2026 worth over $5 billion
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings:

## Summary

**The most important finding is a timing correction:** The Poland K2 **"second executive contract" was signed on August 1, 2025 — not 2026.** It was worth **~$6.5 billion** (180 tanks + 81 support vehicles), signed at Bumar-Łabędy in Gliwice, and was the largest single arms export contract ever won by a South Korean company. If the forecasting question expects this to happen in July–August 2026, that milestone already occurred a year earlier.

**On new deals >$5 billion expected in July–August 2026:** As of early-July 2026 reporting, **no new South Korea arms deal worth over $5 billion was confirmed or clearly imminent** in that specific window. The 2026 focus shifted to industrial/localization agreements (the April 2026 K2PL domestic-production deal with Bumar-Łabędy), rather than fresh mega-procurement signings. Notably, Hanwha Ocean **lost** the major Canadian submarine bid to Germany's TKMS on July 6–7, 2026.

**Framework context:** The 2022 Poland framework covers up to ~1,000 K2 tanks across 6 executive contracts. Contract 1 (2022, $3.7B) and Contract 2 (Aug 2025, $6.5B) are done; Contracts 3–6 (~640 tanks, increasingly K2PL variant) remain but had no confirmed July–August 2026 signing date or value.

**Market scale:** South Korea's 2025 exports hit $15.4B; it's now the world's 9th-largest arms exporter (SIPRI), 2nd-largest supplier to European NATO after the US, with the Big 4 firms holding a ~$69B backlog.

The full structured findings — including all data points and 15 sources — have been submitted via the StructuredOutput tool.

sources.title: Poland doubles down on South Korean tanks with $6.5 billion deal
sources.snippet: Poland ordered 180 K2 Black Panther tanks with 81 accompanying vehicles from Hyundai Rotem; deal worth ~$6.5 billion, signed August 1, 2025 in Gliwice.

sources.title: Hyundai Rotem signs $6.5 billion agreement with Poland for second batch of K2 main battle tanks
sources.snippet: Signed Friday 1 August at Bumar-Łabędy plant in Gliwice; 116 K2GF tanks delivered 2026–2027, 64 K2PL 2028–2030, final assembly of 61 K2PL in Poland.

sources.title: South Korea's Hyundai Rotem concludes deliveries of 180 K2 main battle tanks to Poland
sources.snippet: August 2022 first executive contract valued at $3.7 billion; August 2025 second deal for 116 K2GF + K2PL tanks.

sources.title: Poland orders more K2 MBTs, support vehicles
sources.snippet: The Polish Armaments Agency signed the second executive contract with Hyundai Rotem for 180 more K2 MBTs at Bumar-Łabędy in Gliwice on 1 August.

sources.title: Hyundai Rotem signs local production pact for K2 tanks in Poland
sources.snippet: April 28, 2026: Hyundai Rotem signed a contract with Poland's Bumar-Łabędy to locally manufacture the K2PL variant, part of the second implementation phase.

sources.title: Poland signs agreement to produce South Korean K2 tanks domestically
sources.snippet: April 2026 agreement between Bumar-Łabędy and Hyundai Rotem for domestic K2 production.

sources.title: South Korean tanks for Poland (Defence24 factory report)
sources.snippet: 1,000 tanks ordered across 6 production contracts (excluding engineering vehicles); first contract 2022, all 180 K2s transferred by 2025.

sources.title: S. Korea's Hanwha Ocean loses out as Canada picks Germany's TKMS for submarine deal
sources.snippet: July 2026: Canada selected Germany's TKMS as preferred bidder for its multibillion-dollar submarine project, a setback for Hanwha Ocean.

sources.title: South Korea's Hanwha loses major Canadian submarine deal
sources.snippet: German shipbuilder TKMS chosen as preferred bidder to supply up to 12 vessels; announced July 6, 2026.

sources.title: Korea's Defense Exports Hit $15.4 Billion in 2025, Set to Grow Further
sources.snippet: DAPA: defense exports reached $15.4 billion in 2025; 2026 expected to be even better.

sources.title: South Korea Joins the World's Top 10 Arms-Exporting Nations
sources.snippet: South Korea ranked 9th among world's largest arms exporters, ~3% of global market (SIPRI/Politico, June 2026).

sources.title: South Korea becomes world's fastest-growing arms exporter — 4x jump since 2021
sources.snippet: Big 4 defense firms projected 2026 revenue ~$37 billion; SIPRI data; 4x revenue jump since 2021.

sources.title: Korean Defense Stocks: Why K-Arms Are Europe's Go-To Arsenal 2026
sources.snippet: Big 4 (Hanwha, KAI, Hyundai Rotem, LIG Nex1) built a $69 billion order backlog; exports grew 177% in five years.

sources.title: With $6.7B Contract on K2PLs Signed, Poland's Tank Industry is Set For Revival
sources.snippet: 180 MBTs ordered plus 25 engineering vehicles, 25 bridge-laying vehicles, 31 armored recovery vehicles; $6.7B total.

sources.title: Hanwha Aerospace inks deal to supply K9 howitzers to Romania
sources.snippet: Nearly $1 billion contract with Romania for 54 K9 SPH and 36 K10 ARV over five years.

key_facts: Poland's K2 SECOND executive contract with Hyundai Rotem was signed August 1, 2025 (not 2026), worth ~$6.5 billion for 180 tanks + 81 support vehicles — the largest single arms export contract ever won by a South Korean company.
The FIRST K2 executive contract (August 2022) was worth $3.7 billion for 180 tanks, all delivered by 2025.
The overarching 2022 framework covers up to ~1,000 K2 tanks across 6 production/executive contracts; contracts 3–6 (~640 tanks) remain, increasingly in the K2PL variant.

In April 2026, Hyundai Rotem signed a local-production/technology-transfer agreement with Poland's Bumar-Łabędy for domestic K2PL manufacturing — an industrial agreement, not a new multi-billion procurement contract.
As of early July 2026, NO new South Korea arms deal worth over $5 billion was confirmed or clearly imminent for the July–August 2026 window.
On July 6–7, 2026, Hanwha Ocean LOST the major Canadian submarine deal (up to 12 vessels) to Germany's TKMS — a setback, not a win.
South Korea's 2025 defense exports totaled $15.4 billion (DAPA), which said 2026 would be even higher.

SIPRI (June 2026) ranks South Korea as the world's 9th-largest arms exporter (~3% of global market) and 2nd-largest supplier to European NATO countries after the US; Poland is the largest buyer (~$22B cumulative).
The Big 4 Korean defense firms (Hanwha, Hyundai Rotem, LIG Nex1, KAI) had a ~$69B order backlog and ~$37B projected 2026 combined revenue.
The K2 second contract delivery schedule: 116 K2GF tanks in 2026–2027, 64 K2PL tanks in 2028–2030, with final K2PL assembly in Poland.
